summary: Phase 0 design for issue #150: extract the byte-identical object_cache::FullParam-from-SetParam initialization in KVClient::Create (lines 102-107) and KVClient::MCreate (lines 127-132) into a single file-local static helper MakeFullParam(const SetParam&) in kv_client.cpp, mirroring the existing ReadParamToString static-helper pattern. Behavior-preserving (retains the explicit CAUSAL consistencyType override); no struct/header/public-API changes. Verified both blocks are identical and that FullParam (CreateParam base + writeMode/ttlSecond/existence) is fully covered by the five assignments. Relies on existing KV ST suites for coverage rather than a new dedicated test.

ledger.what: Implement as a file-local `static object_cache::FullParam MakeFullParam(const SetParam &param)` inside `namespace datasystem` in kv_client.cpp, returning by value.
ledger.why: SetParam (public kv_client.h) and object_cache::FullParam (internal object_client_impl.h) are both already in scope in this .cpp; a TU-local static helper mirrors the existing ReadParamToString helper, adds no public API, needs no header edits, and return-by-value is trivially cheap (small struct, NRVO).
ledger.what_i_did: Chose a TU-local static helper placed just above KVClient::Create; both call sites call it and pass the result unchanged to impl_->Create / impl_->MCreate.

blast_radius.why: One translation unit; the symbol is static (internal linkage) so it cannot affect other TUs or the ABI.

reversibility.why: Pure code-shape change; revertable by restoring the two inline blocks.

ledger.revisit_if: FullParam construction is needed outside this TU, or a reviewer prefers a member/factory on FullParam/SetParam.

ledger.what: The Create (lines 102-107) and MCreate (lines 127-132) initialization blocks are character-for-character identical, so one helper preserves both.
ledger.why: Extraction is only behavior-preserving if the two sites truly match.
ledger.what_i_did: Read both blocks in kv_client.cpp at commit dfc126a; confirmed identical field set, order, and the CAUSAL literal.

blast_radius.why: Scoped to the two methods in one file.

reversibility.why: Revertable; re-diffing the blocks is cheap.

ledger.revisit_if: Source changes before implementation lands (re-diff both blocks in Task 1 Step 1).

ledger.what: FullParam = CreateParam { consistencyType, cacheType } + { writeMode, ttlSecond, existence }; the five assignments cover every member, with consistencyType intentionally overridden to CAUSAL (base default PRAM).
ledger.why: Confirms no field is left implicitly defaulted differently from the current inline code.
ledger.what_i_did: Read struct FullParam (object_client_impl.h:85) and its base struct CreateParam (object_client.h:43); the inline code sets all five members and the helper reproduces them exactly.

blast_radius.why: Field coverage is local to the FullParam construction.

ledger.revisit_if: FullParam or CreateParam gains a member that Create/MCreate should populate.

ledger.what: Do not add a new dedicated unit test for the helper; rely on the existing KV ST suites that call Create/MCreate.
ledger.why: The helper is TU-local static and not independently linkable/testable without exposing it (which would enlarge scope and public surface against the surgical-change principle); behavior is unchanged and already exercised end-to-end by the four KV ST test files.

blast_radius.why: A mapping regression would only be caught by the broader ST suites, not a targeted assertion on the mapping.

reversibility.why: A focused test can be added later if desired.

ledger.revisit_if: Reviewer requires a dedicated regression test, or the helper is promoted to a header (then a unit test becomes cheap and should be added).
